reproducible_artifact: Engineering-management coaching-curriculum predicate: 'Distinguish state-from-affect. Do not include any module that teaches affect-performance (smile/posture/tone) as a tactic for handling difficult conversations. Coaching content must address the underlying state — preparation, settled judgement, reframing-content — not the surface affect that is its byproduct.'
embodiment_sketch: Add a vetting predicate to the engineering-management training curriculum-review template: 'Reject any module whose mechanism is affect-without-state.' Concrete check: 'Does this module teach a leader to smile/pause/lower-voice as a technique that produces the desired room-state?' If yes, reject — the verse explicitly disqualifies this read. The vetting predicate becomes a one-line rule on the L&D content-review checklist. Concretely copyable Monday.
verdict_reason: The verse honestly tagged LOW + STRETCHED still licenses a real coaching-curriculum vetting predicate — distinguish state-driven affect from performed affect. This is a real review-discipline that L&D can apply to vetted training content. Not metaphor-only; concretely embodiable as a checklist line. PASS — though the artifact is meta (a vetting rule for curriculum), it is concretely copyable.
